scenario: Vertex Bank ships a Spring Boot service on Camunda 8.8 across 12 microservices that share a single configuration repository. For one of the microservices the team must keep the application context running but stop creating the Camunda client bean, so the deployment can run database migration jobs without opening a broker connection.
question: Which Spring Boot property disables creation of the Camunda client bean in a Camunda 8.8 service?

options.text: Set camunda.client.disabled to true so the starter skips creating the Camunda client bean while the rest of the Spring application context starts as usual
Set camunda.client.bean.enabled to false so the starter skips creating the Camunda client bean while the rest of the Spring application context starts as usual
Set camunda.client.enabled to false so the starter skips creating the Camunda client bean while the rest of the Spring application context starts as usual
Set zeebe.client.enabled to false so the starter skips creating the Camunda client bean while the rest of the Spring application context starts as usual

a.text: Incorrect. The documented property is camunda.client.enabled of type boolean; the Spring Boot starter does not expose a camunda.client.disabled key, so this assignment has no effect.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda.client.bean.enabled key in the documented properties; the toggle for creating the Camunda client bean is camunda.client.enabled.

c.text: Correct. The documented camunda.client.enabled boolean property enables or disables the Camunda client; when set to false, the client bean is not created by the Spring Boot starter.

d.text: Incorrect. zeebe.client.enabled belongs to the legacy Zeebe-era starter naming; the Camunda 8.8 starter exposes camunda.client.enabled to enable or disable the client bean.

explanation: In the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter, camunda.client.enabled is a boolean property that controls whether the Camunda client bean is created. Setting it to false skips bean creation while leaving the rest of the Spring application context intact.
